meta.title: Make Platform PM
meta.description: A technical curriculum for Product Managers working on AI code-generation platforms — runtimes, preview environments, agent infrastructure, evals, observability, and extensibility.

blocks.content:
## What This Curriculum Is

This course prepares product managers to work on the infrastructure beneath modern AI code-generation platforms. The target is fluency deep enough to go toe-to-toe with engineers on architecture tradeoffs — not to become an engineer, but to reason about cost, latency, isolation, reliability, and strategic sequencing with the same rigor engineering uses.

The curriculum uses Figma Make as the running worked example throughout — because it exposes the full surface area of the problem (runtimes, previews, deployments, agents, evals, observability, extensibility) and because its public engineering blog provides citable architectural decisions. Every concept generalizes to v0, Lovable, Bolt, Replit Agent, and the broader category.

## How It Is Structured

Eight levels build on each other:

- **Foundations** — the modern web platform stack (browsers, frameworks, build systems, sandboxes, edge networks)
- **Level 1** — the AI code-generation landscape and competitive architecture
- **Level 2** — code execution and runtimes
- **Level 3** — preview environments and deployment
- **Level 4** — agent infrastructure
- **Level 5** — eval systems and quality gates
- **Level 6** — observability and platform health
- **Level 7** — extensibility and platform strategy

## How Each Module Works

Every module follows an evidence-based learning cycle:

1. **Predict** — activate prior knowledge before reading
2. **Learn** — substantive technical content written for someone arguing tradeoffs, not implementing them
3. **Calibration Check** — mid-module questions to test understanding
4. **Try It Yourself** — hands-on exploration
5. **Explain Back** — force articulation in your own words
6. **Reflect** — connect the technical content to product management judgment
7. **Key Takeaway** — one-sentence crystallization
8. **Connect** — thread to the next module

Each level ends with a **Checkpoint** — an interview-style scenario with a model answer, self-evaluation criteria, and common pitfalls.

## Who Is This For

- PMs moving into AI-adjacent platform roles
- PMs on developer tooling teams adding AI features
- PMs on existing products building AI code-gen or coding-agent features
- Engineers transitioning to platform PM roles who want the PM framing on top of their technical depth

The curriculum assumes basic product management fluency and no prior depth in modern frontend web infrastructure. A Concept Primer at the start covers the vocabulary — Vite, WebContainers, Supabase, HMR, edge functions, MCP — from first principles with analogies from adjacent domains.

## How Long It Takes

With four 2-3 hour sessions per week:

- **Week 1:** Concept Primer + Foundations + Level 1
- **Week 2:** Levels 2–3 (core infrastructure)
- **Week 3:** Levels 4–5 (AI-specific surfaces)
- **Week 4:** Levels 6–7 (observability + platform strategy)

Four weeks to fluency. Bookmarkable as a reference after that.
